description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a long, cylindrical handle and two wide, symmetrical blades extending from either side of the head. The handle appears to be segmented with subtle ridges for improved grip and is uniformly dark in color, likely made of metal or a dense composite. The axe head features two broad, crescent-shaped blades with sharp edges and a pointed spike at the top, giving it both cutting and piercing capabilities. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or engravings. The finish is matte, suggesting a practical tool rather than a ceremonial piece. The object is visually imposing and would be suitable for display, collection, or practical use in scenarios requiring heavy-duty cutting or splitting.
description: A double-headed battle axe with a long, ridged handle and two wide, sharp blades, finished in a matte dark color. The design is robust and functional, with a pointed spike at the top.
description_view_0: The axe is seen edge-on, showing the narrow profile of the blades and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: The double-bladed head is fully visible, revealing the symmetrical crescent shapes and central spike, along with the segmented handle.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, the axe is seen from the side, highlighting the thinness of the blades and the straight handle.
description_view_3: The front of the axe is shown, displaying both blades, the central spike, and the detailed handle grip.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight, cylindrical handle with segmented grip features, likely metal, no visible curvature.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical crescent-shaped blades with a central pointed spike, no beard or poll, functional and robust.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional, with the blade appearing to be fitted and possibly wedged onto the handle.
